habitat: Forest understory; scrub; secondary forest; and densely vegetated habitats
story: The Sri Lanka Shama (Copsychus leggei) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the 待确认 family. It is most commonly observed in Forest understory; scrub; secondary forest; and densely vegetated habitats, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Not Evaluated (NE) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Sri Lanka Shama is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Sri Lanka Shama into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
